label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about the World Bank's 1974 study mentioned in the passage. The question asks what the study found regarding economic growth in some backward countries. The options are A to D, each giving a different level of benefit. First, I need to find the part of the text that talks about this study. Let me scan through the passage again. The relevant part is in the third paragraph where it says, "A major study by the World Bank in 1974 concluded that it is now clear that more than decades of rapid growth in developing countries has been of little benefit to a third of their population." So the study found that the economic growth had little benefit to a third of the population. That would mean that a third got little benefit, while the rest might have had more? But the options are about the overall benefit to the countries' people. Let me check the exact wording again. The study says that the benefit came down to only a small part of the population. The exact phrase is "little benefit to a third of their population." So a third is one-third, which is about 33%. That would mean that two-thirds (66%) might have not been mentioned as getting benefit. The options are A: benefit to a third, B: benefit to two-thirds, C: little benefit to their people, D: no benefit at all. Wait, the question is phrased as "brought _." The study says that the growth was of little benefit to a third of their population. So the majority (two-thirds) might have had some benefit? But the answer options don't directly say that. The options are a bit tricky.
